title.rendered: [Free Webinar] The Untapped Potential of Digital Accessibility for Nonprofits

content.rendered:
Date: Tuesday, June 25, 2024
Time: 1 pm ET / 10 am PT (1 hour)
Cost: Free
Presented by:
• Michael Wilkinson, Product Director at Torchbox
• Quarina Sultana, Head of Digital Engagement at the Royal National Institute for Blind People (RNIB)

Making sure your website is easy to use is not just a regulatory requirement, but also a smart move for your nonprofit organization. If your website is not accessible, your nonprofit might be missing the chance to connect with millions of people, including potential supporters.

This free webinar will highlight the importance of accessibility for users with disabilities and the broader benefits of providing a user-friendly experience for all.

What you will learn:

  • The importance of digital accessibility for nonprofits: beyond compliance, towards inclusivity.
  • Insights into the “Digital Inclusivity Dollar” – understanding the impact of accessibility on user engagement and donor outreach.
  • A case study from the RNIB: actionable lessons from their website accessibility project.
  • Practical tips and recommendations for initiating your organization’s journey towards digital accessibility.
  • How to conduct an accessibility audit: identifying areas for improvement and implementing changes.

  Register Now!

Please Note: This webinar will be recorded. If you can not attend the webinar live, please register and you will be sent a link to the recording 24-48 hours after the webinar is over. Also, visit GoToWebinar’s Attendee System Check for information about how to join and attend this webinar.
